problem: What substrate allows silicon-level performance in fully flexible electronics?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: What substrate allows silicon-level performance in fully flexible electronics? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Two-dimensional. Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Synthesis and characterization with 28 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
